perturbation.original_text: Article 66 The CEO shall be nominated by Party B, and the Deputy CEO shall be nominated by Party A The Chief Financial Officer shall be nominated by Party A, and an Assistant Financial Officer may be nominated by Party B. The Chief Financial Officer must consult with the Assistant Financial Officer, if any, on all important financial matters. Each officer nominated by a party or parties in accordance with this paragraph shall be appointed by the Board of Directors. The other Management Personnel shall be nominated by the CEO and appointed by the Board of Directors. The Board may dismiss any Management Personnel. All replacements for any of the Management Personnel, whether by reason of the retirement, resignation, disability or death of a manager or of the removal of a manager by the Board of Directors or by the Party which nominated him, shall be nominated and appointed in the same manner as the original appointee. Other details of management shall be decided by the CEO.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 66 The CEO shall be nominated by Party B, and the Deputy CEO shall be nominated by Party A The Chief Financial Officer shall be nominated by Party A, and an Assistant Financial Officer may be nominated by Party B. The Chief Financial Officer must consult with the Assistant Financial Officer, if any, on all important financial matters. Each officer nominated by a party or parties in accordance with this paragraph shall be appointed by the Board of Directors. The other Management Personnel shall be nominated by the CEO and appointed by the Board of Directors. The Board may dismiss any Management Personnel. All replacements for any of the Management Personnel, whether by reason of the retirement, resignation, disability or death of a manager or of the removal of a manager by the Board of Advisors, shall be nominated and appointed in the same manner as the original appointee. Other details of management shall be decided by the CEO.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'Board of Directors' to 'Board of Advisors', creates an In-Text Contradiction with Article 54 which defines the Board of Directors as the highest authority of the Joint Venture Company, creates ambiguity of where the real power relies on the company and with contradictory enforcements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 54 The Board of Directors shall be the highest authority of the Joint Venture Company. It shall decide all matters of major importance to the Joint Venture Company. The following matters shall require the unanimous assent of all the directors: o Amendment of the Articles of Association; o Termination and dissolution of the Joint Venture Company; o Merger of the Joint Venture Company with another organization; o Major investment by the Joint Venture Company; o Distribution of profit of the Joint Venture Company; o The recruitment and dismissal of the Senior Management Personnel of the Joint Venture Company; o Transfer of a part of all of either Party's interest in the registered capital of the Joint Venture Company; o Increase and decrease of the Joint Venture Company's registered capital; and o The examination and approval of the annual financial report of the Joint Venture Company.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Board of Directors' with 'Board of Advisors' in Article 66. Article 54 explicitly states that the 'Board of Directors shall be the highest authority of the Joint Venture Company.' By changing Article 66 to give dismissal power to the 'Board of Advisors,' this creates a direct conflict with Article 54, which vests ultimate authority, including potentially dismissal authority through Senior Management Personnel dismissals, in the Board of Directors. This introduces ambiguity and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Article 77 The Joint Venture Company shall adopt a labor contract system. The wages, welfare, labor insurance and other rights and obligations of working personnel and Management Personnel shall be regulated through individual or group labor contracts.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 77 The Joint Venture Company shall adopt a labor contract system. The wages, welfare, labor insurance and other rights and obligations of working personnel and Management Personnel shall be regulated through individual or group employment contracts.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'labor contracts' to 'employment contracts' creates an In-Text Contradiction with Article 76. Where it says the LABOR LAW OF THE PEOPLE'S REPUBLIC OF CHINA and the REGULATIONS OF THE PEOPLE'S REPUBLIC OF CHINA ON LABOR MANAGEMENT IN FOREIGN INVESTMENT ENTERPRISES, creates ambiguity of what law should be consider.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 76 Matters relating to the recruitment, wages, insurance, welfare, dismissal of the staff and workers of the Joint Venture Company shall be handled in accordance with the LABOR LAW OF THE PEOPLE'S REPUBLIC OF CHINA and the REGULATIONS OF THE PEOPLE'S REPUBLIC OF CHINA ON LABOR MANAGEMENT IN FOREIGN INVESTMENT ENTERPRISES and related PRC regulations. The Joint Venture Company's internal labor policies shall be established pursuant to relevant PRC laws and regulations, and approved by the Board of Directors.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'labor contracts' to 'employment contracts' creates an in-text contradiction with Article 76, which specifies that matters relating to staff and workers shall be handled in accordance with the LABOR LAW OF THE PEOPLE'S REPUBLIC OF CHINA and the REGULATIONS OF THE PEOPLE'S REPUBLIC OF CHINA ON LABOR MANAGEMENT IN FOREIGN INVESTMENT ENTERPRISES. The original text aligns with this by using the term 'labor contracts.' However, changing it to 'employment contracts' introduces ambiguity as to which legal framework should apply, potentially conflicting with the specified laws in Article 76. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', where using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them can lead to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Article 116 In the event a dispute arises in connection with the interpretation or implementation of this Contract, the Parties shall attempt in the first instance to resolve such dispute through friendly consultations. If the dispute is not resolved through consultations within sixty (60) days after one Party has served a written notice on the other Party requesting the commencement of consultations, then any Party may refer the dispute to arbitration in Singapore under the auspices of the Singapore International Arbitration Centre in accordance with the rules of that Centre for the time being in force and the provisions of Article 117 of this Contract.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 116 In the event a dispute arises in connection with the interpretation or implementation of this Contract, the Parties shall attempt in the first instance to resolve such dispute through friendly discussions. If the dispute is not resolved through discussions within sixty (60) days after one Party has served a written notice on the other Party requesting the commencement of discussions, then any Party may refer the dispute to arbitration in Singapore under the auspices of the Singapore International Arbitration Centre in accordance with the rules of that Centre for the time being in force and the provisions of Article 117 of this Contract.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'consultations' to 'discussions' creates an In-Text Contradiction with Article 117. Where it talks about 'consultations', creates ambiguity whether it is the same than 'discussions'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 117 There shall be three (3) arbitrators, one (1) of whom shall be appointed by Party A, one (1) of whom shall be appointed by Party B, and one (1) of whom shall be appointed by the arbitration tribunal. The arbitration award shall be final and binding on the Parties. When any dispute occurs and when any dispute is under arbitration, except for the matters under dispute, the Parties shall continue to exercise their other respective rights and fulfil their other respective obligations under this Contract. In any arbitration proceeding, any legal proceeding to enforce any arbitration award and in any legal action between the Parties pursuant to or relating to this Contract, each Party expressly waives the defense of sovereign immunity and any other defence based on the fact or allegation that it is an agency or instrumentality of a sovereign state.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'consultations' with 'discussions'. Article 117 refers to 'consultations'. Since the contract uses both terms but only defines one (potentially neither, based on the prompt), it creates ambiguity and an in-text contradiction, as defined by 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'. It's unclear if 'discussions' and 'consultations' are interchangeable, making Article 117 ambiguous in its reference, and thus contradicting Article 116 after the change.
